nameMeanings.name: verch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Orlo.
German: variant of   Ferch  .

variants.name: Ferch
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

nameMeanings.name: Evan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh (southern): from the personal name Evan, itself from Ieuan, a Welsh derivative of Latin Johannes (English John). See Evans .

variants.name: Bevan
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Iefan ‘son of Evan’, with fused patronymic marker (a)p. Compare Evan and Evans .

variants.name: Heaven
meanings: Welsh: variant of   Evan  .
English: variant of   Haven  .
Irish (Tipperary): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEimhín ‘descendant of Eimhín’, a personal name probably based on a diminutive of éimh ‘swift’, ‘prompt, ready’.

variants.name: Haven
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived ‘(by the) harbor’, from Middle English haven (Old English hæfen ‘haven’).
Welsh (Monmouthshire): variant of   Evan  .
Dutch: variant of Haveman , or possibly a topographic name from haven ‘port, harbour’. See also De Haven .

variants.name: Evans
meanings: Welsh: derivative of Evan , from Efan, Ifan, medieval forms of Ieuan (from Latin Johannes, the source of English John), dating from c. 1500, with the post-medieval patronymic suffix -s. Welsh Sion was a separate borrowing of English John, and Evan (Ieuan) and Sion developed as independent names.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Geza, Jeno, Arpad, Attila, Csaba.

Hungarian: variant of   Papp  , meaning ‘priest (of the Catholic or Orthodox Church)’. This surname is also found in Romania, Slovakia, Croatia, and Serbia, and also among Rusyns (in Serbia and Croatia). See also   Pop   2 and   Popa  .
English: variant of   Pape   1.

variants.name: Papp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Laszlo, Zoltan, Geza, Istvan, Gabor, Miklos, Tibor, Arpad, Denes, Imre, Attila.
German (Bavaria, Tyrol): from Middle High German pap ‘baby food’, Dutch pap ‘porridge, pap’, hence perhaps a nickname denoting someone who had lost his teeth or subsisted on a meager diet.

Hungarian: from pap ‘priest (of the Catholic or Orthodox Church)’, probably an occupational name for someone in the service of a priest or a nickname for a pious person. This surname is also found in Romania and Slovakia, and also among Rusyns. Compare Pap and Pop .

nameMeanings.name: Meredith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Meredydd (Old Welsh Morgetiud, Middle Welsh Maredudd, from a possible British Celtic ethnic name of the type Morgetes + iǖth ‘lord’). Compare Beddow .

variants.name: Beddow
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Bedo, a pet form of Mareddud (see Meredith ).

variants.name: Mallalieu
meanings: English (Lancashire, Yorkshire and Cheshire): variant of Meredith . The form of the surname was altered first to Merridew (see Merrihew ), then to Mallalieu, altered to imply Norman French origin. Compare Melody .

variants.name: Meridith
meanings: Welsh: variant of   Meredith  .

variants.name: Merrihew
meanings: English and Irish: most probably an altered form of Welsh Meredith (which is found as Meriday in 16th and 17th century English sources), or possibly of English Mayhew . Compare Mallalieu .

variants.name: Merideth
meanings: Welsh: variant of   Meredith  .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: Jane verch Evan ap Meredith was born in 1395, in Wales. She had at least 1 son with Jenkin Glynn. She died on 17 May 1421, in Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Wales, at the age of 26.

fr.lifeSummary: Jane verch Evan ap Meredith est née en 1395, à Galles. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Jenkin Glynn. Elle est décédée le 17 mai 1421, à Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Galles, à l’âge de 26 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Jane verch Evan ap Meredith wurde 1395 in Wales geboren. Sie hatte mit Jenkin Glynn mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 17. Mai 1421 im Alter von 26 Jahren in Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Wales.

it.lifeSummary: Jane verch Evan ap Meredith è nata il nel 1395 nel Galles. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Jenkin Glynn. Morì il 17 maggio 1421, nel Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Galles, all'età di 26 anni.

ja.Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Wales: ウェールズモントゴメリシュアイアモントゴメリー
ja.lifeSummary: Jane verch Evan ap Meredithは1395年にウェールズで生まれた。彼女はJenkin Glynnとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1421年5月17日にウェールズモントゴメリシュアイアモントゴメリーで26歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane verch Evan ap Meredith(은)는 1395년 웨일스에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Jenkin Glynn(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1421년 5월 17일 웨일스 몽고머이실르 몽고메리에서 26세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jane verch Evan ap Meredith nasceu em 1395, em Gales. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Jenkin Glynn. Ela faleceu em 17 de maio de 1421, em Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Gales, com 26 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Jane verch Evan ap Meredith родилась в 1395 г. в местности Уэльс. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Jenkin Glynn. Она умерла 17 мая 1421 г. в местности монтгомири, монтгомиришир, Уэльс в возрасте 26 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Jane verch Evan ap Meredith nació en 1395, en Gales. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Jenkin Glynn. Murió el 17 de mayo de 1421, en Montgomery, Montgomeryshire, Gales, a la edad de 26 años.
